calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-26-2018 Dismissal - No Appearance

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Continued To Feb-26-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jessica L. Clouse of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415-986-5900) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS (and Cross-defendants), HPS DEVELOPMENT CO. LP, and HPS1 BLOCK 54, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication on all causes of action alleged in plaintiff Timothy Jones' complaint and cross-complainant Alex Prince's cross-complaint is GRANTED. The HPS defendants satisfy their initial summary judgment burden by presenting evidence that all traffic and safety obligations for the Project were delegated to the general contractor Roberts-Obayashi and the HPS defendants did not owe or assume a duty of care to Mr. Jones. Scott Smith (President of Roberts-Obayashi) attaches a copy of the general contract and admits that Roberts-Obayashi was solely responsible for all traffic control. (Smith Dec. par. 12.) Neither Mr. Prince nor Mr. Jones presents competent evidence showing that the HPS defendants negligently retained control of the outer perimeter and affirmatively contributed to Mr. Jones's injury. (Hooker v. Dept. of Transportation (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198.) The HPS defendants' objection to Mr. Jones' deposition testimony at 119:16-120:5 (where he asserts that HPS1 controlled the outer perimeter) is sustained. Other deposition testimony shows that Mr. Jones has no foundation to make that assertion. (Jones Depo. 118:1-119:15.) At most, Mr. Prince and Mr. Jones show that the HPS defendants may have maintained some sort of general supervisory role, but that retention is insufficient to create liability under Hooker. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com (L&M302)
